notes: Builds AIRA (a 15-check deterministic static-analysis tool grounded in a proposed 'Reward-Shaped Failure Hypothesis' framework), then empirically validates it via three studies: a bounded enterprise environment audit (case-study flavor, Study 1) and two matched-control corpus comparisons mining AI-attributed vs. human-authored public repo files from the AIDev dataset and GitHub PRs (repository-mining/MSR + measurement-study, Studies 2-3), plus a secondary LLM-vs-static evaluator comparison. No human participants; human-written code is used only as a comparison baseline. Not a controlled experiment (observational, no manipulation) and not a curated-task benchmark.
